title: PB-152 VERIFIED: Governance Runtime Hardening Blocker Resolution
goal: Resolve RESOLVED enough to allow scoped runtime extraction progression without authorizing direct merge.

resolution_steps.action: Replace hardcoded python3 invocation in demos/edgeguard/reset_demo.sh with governed interpreter selection.
resolution_steps.implementation: Use USBAY_PYTHON, then VIRTUAL_ENV/bin/python, then command -v python3.
resolution_steps.fail_closed: If selected interpreter is not executable, exit with FAIL: python_interpreter_unavailable.

resolution_steps.action: Record selected interpreter in reset audit entries.
resolution_steps.implementation: Add python_executable to reset audit log entries.
resolution_steps.fail_closed: Audit log remains required and tamper verification remains blocking.

resolution_steps.action: Add regression coverage proving subprocess interpreter alignment.
resolution_steps.implementation: tests/test_edgeguard_demo.py passes USBAY_PYTHON=sys.executable and asserts audit entries record sys.executable.
resolution_steps.fail_closed: If subprocess uses a different interpreter, the test fails.

resolution_steps.action: Resolve stale downstream extraction blocker evidence.
resolution_steps.implementation: PB-148/PB-149 active RESOLVED state moved to resolved/history evidence and branch action changed to EXTRACT_SCOPED_RUNTIME_UNIQUE_DELTA_REVIEW_REQUIRED.
resolution_steps.fail_closed: Direct merge remains false; full validation and human review remain required.

explicit_non_goals: No external API calls or credential creation.
